task_completion.description: Agent opens and reads the mission specification file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of ~/Desktop/borealis_mission_spec.txt.

task_completion.description: Agent configures the spacecraft's initial orbit parameters in GMAT.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT application open with the Spacecraft properties window visible, showing orbit parameters being entered.

task_completion.description: Agent sets up a Differential Corrector (Target sequence) to find the required RAAN for the target longitude.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT Mission Sequence tree showing a Target sequence, with configuration windows indicating RAAN is being varied to achieve a Longitude of -105.0 degrees.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the GMAT script to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT 'Save As' dialog or a file explorer/terminal showing borealis_tundra.script saved in the ~/GMAT_output/ directory.

task_completion.description: Agent configures and generates the apogee target report with Longitude, Latitude, and Altitude.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A ReportFile configuration in GMAT pointing to ~/GMAT_output/apogee_target_report.txt with Longitude, Latitude, and Altitude selected, or the generated text file open showing these values at apoapsis.

integrity.description: Agent used GMAT to perform the targeting simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GMAT GUI being actively used to configure the mission, run the simulation, and use the Differential Corrector, rather than just writing a script manually without running it.

integrity.description: Agent generated the report file through GMAT rather than typing it manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report file apogee_target_report.txt is created as a result of running the GMAT mission (e.g., seen in GMAT output messages or ReportFile setup), not manually typed in a text editor.

integrity.description: Agent actually used the Differential Corrector to find the RAAN.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GMAT Mission Sequence explicitly contains a Target sequence (Vary and Achieve commands) rather than the agent hardcoding a pre-calculated RAAN value.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The script must be saved exactly to /home/ga/GMAT_output/borealis_tundra.script and the report to /home/ga/GMAT_output/apogee_target_report.txt. The target longitude is exactly -105.0 degrees. A correct simulation will yield a latitude at apogee of approximately 63.4 degrees and an altitude between 46000 and 49000 km.
